plan_metadata.name: DA Api Key ts Remote Debug

steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Add an Action" option in the dropdown menu within the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog in Visual Studio Code (Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension).
Click the "Start with a New API" option in the "Create an Action" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel in Visual Studio Code.
Select the "API Key" option from the "Authentication Type" dropdown menu in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "TypeScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown within the VS Code interface.
Click the "Default folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" dropdown within the Visual Studio Code application to select the project directory at /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
cd /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_b3fM7 && npm run keygen
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
> vscuseappb3fm7@1.0.0 keygen
> node -r ts-node/register ./src/keyGen.ts

Generated a new API Key: zxq/KAjTtptd

✓ Script executed successfully
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
ENV_FILE="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_b3fM7/env/.env.dev.user"
API_KEY="zxq/KAjTtptd"
# Verify the env file exists
if [ -f "$ENV_FILE" ]; then
  # Use sed to replace the SECRET_API_KEY line
  sed -i "s|SECRET_API_KEY=.*|SECRET_API_KEY='$API_KEY'|" "$ENV_FILE"
  echo "Updated .env.dev.user file:"
  cat "$ENV_FILE"
else
  echo ".env.dev.user file does not exist at $ENV_FILE" >&2
  exit 1
fi
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
Updated .env.dev.user file:
# This file includes environment variables that will not be committed to git by default. You can set these environment variables in your CI/CD system for your project.

# Secrets. Keys prefixed with `SECRET_` will be masked in Teams Toolkit logs.
SECRET_API_KEY='zxq/KAjTtptd'

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description: @code run "npm run keygen" at /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}. fill in the "Generated a new API Key: <value>" into /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/env/.env.dev.user, replace SECRET_API_KEY= ' ' with the  <value> and save the file.
Click the "env" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to expand its contents.
Click the ".env.dev.user" file in the "env" folder within the VS Code Explorer sidebar.
Perform a right-click on the tab header of the file `.env.dev.user` within the VS Code editor interface at coordinates (374, 52).
Click the "Close" option in the context menu of the file `.env.dev.user` in Visual Studio Code's Explorer panel.
